verdict:
PROVEN — a tiling-pattern-filled PDF was actually produced, and the pattern lands in the RIGHT PLACE. Not claimed from reading source: verified three independent ways.

WHAT WORKS (all five requirements met, all in one PDF):
(0) Pattern defined inside advancedAPI(), filled from the app's normal COMPAT mode (mm, Y-down) — 5x "/Pattern cs" + "scn" + "f*" in the raw uncompressed stream.
(1) Non-axis-aligned slanted parallelogram, even-odd fill.
(a) Rotated pattern (30deg) — emitted /Matrix [2.4549 -1.4173 -1.4173 -2.4549 396.85 538.587] matches the analytic prediction to 10 significant figures.
(b) World-origin anchoring — pixel-probed: 9/9 mid-band samples at x=60mm match the predicted stripe phase ((y-20)%6); the only "mismatches" are samples landing exactly on phase 0.0/3.0, which return antialiasing blends, i.e. the boundary is exactly where the math says.
(c) Two shapes reusing one definition — works, but see the honest caveat below.
(d) Image-cell pattern (addImage inside the cell) — works with no special handling; /XObject /I0 is auto-included in the pattern's own /Resources, and pdfjs resolves it to paintImageXObject.

WHAT DID NOT WORK / MUST BE REPORTED HONESTLY:
1. DEDUP (requirement c) only half-works. Two shapes DO share one pattern object ('/P1 scn' twice, zero clones) — but ONLY by omitting patternData.matrix, and that forfeits all placement control: with no /Matrix the pattern space collapses to POINTS, Y-up from the page bottom. I proved this by probing shape C: the 6-unit cell rendered as 6pt (2.117mm) stripes phase-locked to points-from-bottom. So there is NO way to both dedup AND anchor/rotate/scale. Any fill that supplies a matrix mints a permanent new clone with a full copy of the cell stream. For a topo surface with many faces and an image-cell material, that duplicates the raster per fill. Workable, but the caller must memoise clones itself — this is the main engineering cost of the approach and should be decided before building on it.
2. The unit ambiguity flagged as unresolvable from source alone (Q6#5) is now RESOLVED empirically: jsPDF's flip matrix is genuinely unit-wrong (mm-valued page height where the PDF spec demands points). It is survivable only because the flip is an involution and can be cancelled exactly. This makes the matrix helper load-bearing and non-obvious — open-coding it per call site will produce silently misplaced patterns ~74mm off.
3. My first attempt to raster-verify failed (pdfjs throws 'InvalidArg' on pattern fills in Node without a canvasFactory). Fixed by supplying one; noting it because a CI raster assertion would hit the same wall.

Confidence: high on everything asserted above (each claim traces to emitted bytes, a pdfjs operator list, or a pixel probe). The one thing I did NOT test is behaviour at production scale — many patterns/fills on a real topo surface, where the O(n^2) resource dicts and per-fill stream duplication are unmeasured. I would not assume that is fine without a volume test.

Note: spike-tiling.mjs, spike-out.pdf and spike-out.png are untracked scratch artifacts at the repo root; delete them or move them out before any commit (I made no commits, per N.-1, and ran no tsc, per N.17).
gotchas: THE mm-vs-POINTS FLIP BUG IS REAL (prior research Q6#5 confirmed, and it is NOT a blocker). fillWithPattern builds F = Matrix(1,0,0,-1,0,getPageHeight()) where getPageHeight() returns mm (210.0016), but a PDF pattern /Matrix must map pattern space to DEFAULT USER SPACE, which is POINTS. So jsPDF's flip is unit-wrong by construction for any non-'pt' doc. It is harmless ONLY because F is an involution and you can cancel it exactly: pass patternData.matrix = F.multiply(M_final). If you naively pass a sane mm->page matrix without cancelling F, the pattern lands ~74mm off (210 points instead of 210 mm from the bottom). Every caller MUST route through one helper (patternMatrixFor) — this is not something to open-code per call site.

DEDUP AND PLACEMENT CONTROL ARE MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE. Omitting patternData.matrix is the ONLY way to reuse the original pattern object (proved: shapes C both emit '/P1 scn', no clone). But then /Matrix is omitted from the dict => pattern space === default user space === POINTS, Y-UP from the bottom-left. Pixel-probed and confirmed: the same 6-unit cell renders as 6pt (2.117mm) stripes, phase-locked to points-from-page-bottom, not to mm-from-top. So the 'dedup' path is useless for any real anchored/scaled material. If you supply a matrix, cloneTilingPattern registers a BRAND-NEW pattern object on EVERY fill call, never cached (proved: 5 fills / 2 definitions -> 5 pattern objects P1..P5, with P3,P4 stripe clones and P5 a photo clone). Each clone writes a FULL COPY of the cell stream into the PDF. For an image-cell material this means the raster stream is duplicated per fill — a real size bomb for a topo surface with many faces. Mitigation if this ships: dedup clones yourself by memoising on a (key + matrix.toString() + bbox + steps) cache key and reusing the returned pattern id, or accept one clone per distinct material+placement.

RESOURCE DICTS GROW O(n^2). jsPDF gives every pattern its own /Resources dict containing ALL 14 standard fonts, ALL /XObject images, AND every pattern defined so far (obj 27's /Pattern dict lists P1,P2,P3,P4). With N patterns you get N dicts each listing up to N patterns. Fine at 5, needs measuring before shipping hundreds.

In COMPAT mode you MUST pass style === null to lines()/rect() to suppress the paint op. Passing undefined falls through to getStyle(undefined) -> defaultPathOperation 'S' and strokes the path. pdf.path() is always paint-op-free in both modes and is the safer builder.

The cell content stream is authored in ADVANCED mode, so coordinates are RAW (no scaleFactor, no Y-flip) — but the cell's Y axis is UP (PDF native), while the page-mm world is Y-down. In the spike the /Matrix's negative `d` term flips the cell back, so cell-local v reads as DOWN on the page and the stripe order comes out as authored. Anyone hand-rolling a different matrix must keep that sign or the cell renders mirrored.

getPageHeight() for A4 landscape returns 210.0015555555555, not 210 — floating slop from the pt->mm round trip. Harmless here because the same value is used to build and to cancel F, but do not hardcode 210 in the helper or the cancellation stops being exact.

Pattern definition requires ADVANCED mode (advancedApiModeTrap on beginTilingPattern/endTilingPattern), but filling has ZERO apiMode gate — confirmed by execution, not just by reading. patterns/patternMap are instance-scoped and untouched by advancedAPI()/compatAPI(), so define-in-advanced / fill-in-compat is a supported combination in practice.

fillWithPattern silently no-ops on an unregistered/mistyped key (patterns[undefined] matches neither instanceof branch) — no throw, no operator emitted. A typo'd key produces an invisible unfilled shape, not an error. Any wrapper should assert the key is registered.

Rendering this PDF with pdfjs in Node requires a custom canvasFactory (@napi-rs/canvas); without one, tiling-pattern fills throw 'InvalidArg' inside CanvasGraphics.eoFill. That is a pdfjs/Node limitation, NOT a defect in the produced PDF — relevant only if a test wants to raster-assert pattern output in CI.
